channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In an engineering feat, mechanical SpaceX arms catch Starship rocket booster back at the launch pad
item.description: SpaceX pulled off the boldest test flight yet of its enormous Starship rocket on Sunday, catching the returning booster back at the launch pad with mechanical arms. A jubilant Elon Musk called it “science fiction without the fiction part.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ukraine’s human rights envoy urges response to alleged killings of Ukrainian POWs in Kursk
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s human rights ombudsman urged international organizations Sunday to respond to a claim that several Ukrainian prisoners of war were executed in Russia’s Kursk region, where Kyiv had launched an incursion in August.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israeli strikes kill a family of 8 in Gaza and destroy a century-old market in Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on the central Gaza Strip killed a family of eight, Palestinian medical officials said Sunday, as Israeli forces battled militants in the territory's north and airstrikes in pursuit of Hezbollah destroyed a century-old market in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden views Hurricane Milton damage in Florida
item.description: President Joe Biden on Sunday will survey the devastation inflicted on Florida's Gulf Coast by Hurricane Milton as he urges Congress to approve additional emergency disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on insults and mockery in attacks on Harris
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday called Vice President Harris a “dummy” after she appeared with what he called the “degenerates on The View.” On Wednesday, he mockingly compared Harris giving interviews to the sound of a vacuum and derided her as “grossly incompetent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Professor leaves university after 'highly inappropriate' remarks during lecture
item.description: A University of Kansas professor has left the university after a video went viral on X showing the professor making unnerving remarks
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tested the limits on using the military at home. If elected again, he plans to go further
item.description: During his first term as president, Donald Trump tested the limits of how he could use the military to achieve policy goals.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iowa mom's boyfriend nearly got away with murder — until a zipper changed everything
item.description: Dr. Michael Baden recounts how a nondescript zipper imprint helped prosecutors put Ivan "Sam" Brammer behind bars in the death of his girlfriend, Ilene Gowan, in a new Fox Nation show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina’s mountains, the roar of gas-powered generators is both unpleasant and essential.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Aoun Angueira, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Determination to rebuild follows Florida's hurricanes with acceptance that storms will come again
item.description: The devastation of Hurricanes Helene and Milton is still being tallied as a swath of Florida comes to terms with damage from the unusual dual strike of storms in such close proximity.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum, Brendan Farrington And Ty Oneil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOJ lawsuit filed against Virginia for voter list purge
item.description: With Election Day just around the corner, the Commonwealth is in hot water after the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced it is suing the state for illegally striking voters off of voter lists.
item.dc:creator: Allison Williams, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado family trying to get dad home after falling ill on European cruise
item.description: A Colorado family is desperately trying to get their dad from Englewood home after a sudden and severe illness in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Kerridge, NewsNation

item.title: Raleigh man arrested in connection to 1998 sexual assault cold case: Police
item.description: Raleigh police said a man has been arrested in connection to a sexual assault that occurred over 25 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Glyniss Wiggins, NewsNation

item.title: Fidelity data breach exposed info from 77,000 customers
item.description: Thousands of Fidelity Investments customers may have had their personal information stolen amid a data breach that occurred in August of this year, the financial firm announced.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Florida deputies help pregnant woman in labor during Milton
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said that some of the office's deputies helped transport a pregnant woman who was going into labor to the hospital early Thursday morning, as Hurricane Milton brought widespread power outages, high winds and flooding to Central Florida.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic League accuses Whitmer of mocking Eucharist with Dorito
item.description: Podcaster Liz Plank posted a video on her Instagram showing herself being fed a Dorito by Whitmer, who's standing above Plank.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky woman arrested after dismembered body parts found 'cooked' inside oven
item.description: A witness told state police that she was reportedly "casting spells" and being "confrontational" the day prior to the discovery.
item.dc:creator: Jace O'Barto, NewsNation

item.title: Asheville restaurant owners trying to recover from Helene
item.description: “Seeing the community come together like it has. It’s been incredibly heartwarming,” said Bryan King, co-owner of 12 Bones Smokehouse and Brewing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Charlotte school recovering from fire holds benefit concert for Helene victims
item.description: Strings of support. On Friday, Charlotte Preparatory School, a school still recovering from a massive 2023 fire, held a benefit concert for Helene victims.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Pierce, NewsNation

item.title: Thomas hits grand slam off Skubal, Guardians down Tigers 7-3 to reach AL Championship Series
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — For the third time in less than a month, the Cleveland Guardians made a semi-circle in the middle of their clubhouse and emptied champagne and beer bottles on each other as “Rocky Top” blasted through the speakers. The choice of music is unique. So is this team.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: North Carolina football player Tylee Craft dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: North Carolina football player Tylee Craft died Saturday morning from a rare form of lung cancer, coach Mack Brown said. “This young man fought so hard for his two and a half years,” Brown said. “The doctors told us he outlived what he should’ve.
item.dc:creator: DOUG BONJOUR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Historic ocean liner could soon become the world's largest artificial reef
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The conservancy that oversees a storied but aging ocean liner and its landlord have resolved a years-old rent dispute that will clear the way for a Florida county to turn the historic ship into the world’s largest artificial reef.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UCLA professor says he’s homeless due to low pay
item.description: (KTLA) -- In a now-viral TikTok video, Dr. Daniel McKeown, a professor at UCLA, claims low pay has left him homeless, shocking many of his followers. "Hi everyone, my name is Daniel, and I’m an astrophysics professor at UCLA.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, ABC 4

item.title: Which states have the highest and lowest voter turnout in presidential elections?
item.description: The rate of voter turnout for the 2020 presidential election was around 67%. But some states were really bringing up that average.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Inspectors find bed bugs at Bellagio, Cosmopolitan hotels in Las Vegas
item.description: A report reveals health inspectors found bed bugs at some prominent hotels on the Las Vegas Strip during a six-month period.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, ABC 4

item.title: Harris outpaces Trump in battleground Pennsylvania, but trails in Arizona: Poll
item.description: The New York Times/Philadelphia Inquirer/Siena College survey, released Saturday, shows Harris leading Trump 50 percent to 47 percent among likely voters in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, WGN 9

item.title: Should you get the COVID and flu shots in the same arm? Here's what studies show
item.description: The peak respiratory virus season is fast approaching, and public health officials are recommending you roll up your sleeves — or maybe just one sleeve?
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Professor leaves KU after 'highly inappropriate' remarks during lecture
item.description: A University of Kansas professor has left the university after a video went viral on X showing the professor making unnerving remarks
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UCLA professor says he’s homeless due to low pay
item.description: (KTLA) -- In a now-viral TikTok video, Dr. Daniel McKeown, a professor at UCLA, claims low pay has left him homeless, shocking many of his followers. "Hi everyone, my name is Daniel, and I’m an astrophysics professor at UCLA.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Allred pushes back on Cruz transgender attack ads
item.description: “Ted Cruz is lying again, but now he's lying about our children. I'm a dad. I'm also a Christian,” Allred said on camera.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trial on hold for New Jersey man charged in knife attack that injured Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The attempted murder trial of the man charged with severely injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack was put on hold Friday while judges consider a request to move it to another county. Jury selection had been scheduled to start on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee authorities dispose large box with dynamite that prompted evacuations
item.description: A box filled with dynamite found at a metal recycling plant in Tennessee was being handled by police, who are trying to dispose of the contents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Residents begin repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which killed at least 10
item.description: Florida residents began repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a deadly tornadoes. At least 10 people are dead, but many expressed relief that Milton wasn’t worse.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 in 5 millennials want full access to abortion: Study
item.description: The largest percentile of individuals who do not want to live in a "fully legal" abortion state is Gen Z at 33%, followed by Gen X and Baby Boomers (30%).
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KRON4

item.title: Woman accused of killing, cooking her mother
item.description: Warning: the story below contains disturbing details. A Kentucky woman is facing charges after police responded to a report of a dismembered body and found pieces of the woman's mother cooking inside a pot on the stove.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4
